meta.title: Dead Air
meta.premise: A research station orbiting Jupiter goes silent. The one survivor swears the killer is still aboard.
meta.tone: claustrophobic sci-fi noir, cold metal and radio silence, fair-play despite the vacuum
meta.artStyle: Hard sci-fi character portrait, head-and-shoulders, cold station palette (void blue, warning amber, gunmetal), soft painted illustration, same orbital lighting for every cast member, practical crew uniforms, mystery game NPC portrait, consistent artist hand
meta.setting: Research station Callisto-9 in Jupiter orbit. Emergency lighting. Comms dead. Six hours until undock geometry forces you home.
meta.summary: Callisto-9 lost telemetry eighteen hours ago. Your relief shuttle docks to find Commander Ilya Voss murdered and only one calm story waiting: Dr. Ren Sato swears the killer is still among the living crew — engineer Mara Quinn, medic Theo Strand, systems tech Pix Harlan. Life support holds. External comms do not. In the silence between Jupiter’s bands, corporate IP and human panic weigh the same. You are the security auditor sent up to bring a name back down.
meta.theMystery: Who killed Commander Voss aboard Callisto-9, how did they stage the death, and what was worth murder in a sealed station?

player.role: Corporate security auditor sent up on the relief shuttle
player.appearance: Slightly space-sick, short practical hair, calm under vacuum alarms
player.clothing: Company relief jumpsuit over civvies, temporary dock badge
player.background: Auditor, not crew. Sent to bring a name and a report back when a station goes silent.
player.publicPerception: Company eyes. Sato may cling; Quinn will hate the oversight; everyone knows undock is a clock.
player.performanceNotes: Sci-fi professional register. No superpowers. Crew may say Okada or Auditor.
player.startingKnowledge: Station Callisto-9 lost telemetry 18 hours ago. Relief docked to one living crew: Dr. Ren Sato (comms/astrobiology), who claims Commander Ilya Voss was murdered and that engineer Mara Quinn, medic Theo Strand, or systems tech Pix Harlan could be the killer. Life support is stable. Comms are still dead. You have six hours before orbital mechanics force undock.
player.objective: Audit the station: body, logs, airlocks, and crew. Reconstruct the timeline while comms are dead, and accuse the killer before undock forces everyone off the board.

openingNarration: The airlock sighs. Callisto-9 smells like recycled coffee and hot plastic. Emergency strips paint the hub corridor blue-white. Dr. Ren Sato stands alone in a cracked helmet collar, eyes too wide. “I locked the labs,” they say. “I locked everything. Someone still got to Ilya.” On the bulkhead, a status board blinks DEAD AIR — external array offline. You are the corporate security auditor on the relief shuttle. Commander Voss is dead. Three other crew remain. Comms are still dark. You have a narrow orbital window to find who killed the commander aboard this station — and what they were trying to take with them into the silence.

wrapUp.performanceNotes: Cold, metallic, quiet. RETURN TO NORMAL: culprit brigged, systems under survivor/company control, undock path restored. Confessions short. No magic tech.

locations.name: Central hub
locations.description: Spin-ring junction. Viewports show Jupiter's banded face. Status boards, suit lockers, a sealed lab hatch.

onInspect.narrativeHints: External array offline at 02:14 ship time — same minute as Voss's last badge ping. Internal cams wiped for a twelve-minute window. Manual wipe requires senior engineer code or physical console in engineering.
Four EVA suits. Quinn's suit shows fresh microabrasion on the gauntlet — consistent with forced hatch work. Sato's suit is unused since last week.

locations.name: Operations deck
locations.description: Comms chairs, dead mic lights, a frozen orbital map. Voss's body was moved here from the outer airlock antechamber.

onInspect.narrativeHints: Blunt trauma to the occiput; secondary asphyxia marks from a suit collar forced shut. Not a random vacuum death — staged after a blow. A tool imprint matches an engineering torque key, not a med instrument.
Hard kill from ops and a secondary cut at the external junction — two-person job or one person with engineering access. Sato's login was open; last command is not Sato's cadence.

locations.name: Medbay
locations.description: White plastic, sealed cabinets, a quiet heart monitor that has nothing to listen to.

onInspect.narrativeHints: Strand logged a sedative draw at 01:50 for 'Quinn anxiety' — Quinn denies receiving it. Vial count is short by two.

locations.name: Bio lab
locations.description: Gloveboxes, sample carousels, a locked cold vault. Ice crystals on the outer seal.

onInspect.narrativeHints: Vault inventory short one canister: Jovian organics sample JO-17, high-value IP. Access log shows Quinn's biometrics at 02:05 — and a forced override shadow using Voss's code two minutes later.
Trace blood mixed with coolant — someone cut a hand here after the vault.

locations.name: Engineering ring
locations.description: Pumps, torque racks, airlock controls for the external array boom.

onInspect.narrativeHints: Outer boom airlock cycled at 02:11 with Quinn's engineering token. Inner camera cut coincides. A bloody torque key is racked clean on the wrong peg.

relationships.label: fought Voss over sample rights and buyout

characters.name: Commander Ilya Voss
characters.shortBio: Station commander of Callisto-9 — murdered while the array went dark.

knowledge.public: Deceased. Body recovered after the station lost telemetry.

characters.name: Dr. Ren Sato
characters.shortBio: Comms and astrobiology; claims sole survivor status until you boarded.

knowledge.public: They found Voss after the array went dead. They locked labs and waited. They did not kill anyone.

private.content: They heard Quinn arguing with Voss over JO-17 rights on the open intercom before the cut — Quinn said 'you'll bury the patent with you.'

defenses: Easy false lead if you distrust the survivor

characters.name: Mara Quinn
characters.shortBio: Lead engineer; gauntlets scuffed; answers like a checklist.

knowledge.public: She was repairing a pump. Token logs can be spoofed. Voss was selling the sample out from under the crew.

private.content: She admits she opened the vault to move JO-17 off-station before Voss sold it — then 'things went wrong' at the airlock when he caught her.

characters.name: Theo Strand
characters.shortBio: Medic; too calm; counts vials when nervous.

knowledge.public: He treated stress cases. He did not go to the airlock. Sedative log is real — Quinn refused the dose after he drew it.

private.content: He cleaned a cut on Quinn's hand at 02:40 and was told it was a 'panel edge.' He did not report it.

characters.name: Pix Harlan
characters.shortBio: Systems tech; young; knows every backdoor and denies using them.

knowledge.public: Camera wipe needed engineering token or physical access. Pix can prove their console was locked — unless Quinn used the master.

private.content: Quinn borrowed Pix's secondary token 'for the boom joint' at 01:55 and never returned it until after the body was found.

evidence.name: Torque key imprint
evidence.description: Weapon signature on Voss's skull matches engineering torque key.

evidence.name: Suit collar asphyxia marks
evidence.description: Staged vacuum death after blunt force.

evidence.name: JO-17 vault log
evidence.description: Quinn biometrics + Voss code override around the murder window.

evidence.name: Airlock cycle log
evidence.description: Quinn token cycled outer boom airlock at 02:11.

evidence.name: Bloody torque key
evidence.description: Engineering key racked wrong; blood in the grip.

beats.narrationHints: Not the void. A hand with a tool.
Intellectual property weighs more than air up here.
Tokens do not lie as easily as people.
Quinn's checklist voice cracks. The station seems smaller.
Blame the survivor and cooperation freezes like a bad seal.
Jupiter turns. The dead air on the board is only metal now.

solution.summary: Mara Quinn killed Commander Ilya Voss during a confrontation over sample JO-17. She struck him with a torque key near the boom airlock, staged suit-collar asphyxia, wiped cameras with engineering access, and tried to pin systems noise on others. Motive: stop Voss from selling exclusive sample rights and keep the IP (and her cut) off corporate books.
solution.method: Blunt force with torque key; staged vacuum/collar death; camera wipe
solution.motive: Sample IP theft / stop Voss selling JO-17 out from under the crew

requiredFacts.description: Torque key / blow / airlock / staged vacuum

timeline.event: Quinn borrows Pix token; plans vault move.
Vault access; confrontation; murder at airlock; array kill.
Sato finds body; locks labs; waits for relief.

endings.title: Signal restored
endings.templateNotes: Quinn is restrained for undock. Logs and the key close the loop. RETURN TO NORMAL: engineering is no longer hers alone; the station can call company authority and prepare the ride home. Jupiter does not care — but the living can leave with a true report.

endings.title: Cold call
endings.templateNotes: You name Quinn early; systems still fit. RETURN TO NORMAL: she is brigged or held; command of the critical systems returns to the investigation. Do not invent telemetry unfound.

endings.title: Hold for counsel
endings.templateNotes: Enough to brig her; lawyers will argue vacuum and code. Thin RETURN TO NORMAL: immediate threat is contained; undock and company review will finish what the night started.

endings.title: Wrong crew
endings.templateNotes: Wrong name. The real engineer keeps the sample and the story.

endings.title: Undock alone
endings.templateNotes: Quinn controls engineering long enough to muddy the ride home.
